description: US-06 Check the Certificate and Log a Reminder — Certificate expiry never blocks a hand-out; reminders are logged at most once per day; reaching the threshold prompts — never forces — archiving.

userStories.title: Certificate validity and reminder escalation rules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the expiry and escalation rules as pure functions, so the grace period behaves identically everywhere. Source: tasks/prd-us-06-certificate-reminder.md §US-06.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/certificate.ts exports isExpired(certificate, today)
A certificate is valid on its validUntil date and expired the day after — tested for the day before, the day of and the day after
escalation(reminderCount, threshold, expired) returns NONE, REMIND or PROMPT_ARCHIVE
PROMPT_ARCHIVE is returned only when the count has reached the threshold AND the certificate is still expired
A valid certificate always yields NONE regardless of the reminder count
today and threshold are parameters — no clock and no settings lookup in the module
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: recordReminder and renewCertificate use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to log a reminder and record a renewed certificate, so the escalation is documented rather than remembered. Source: tasks/prd-us-06-certificate-reminder.md §US-06.2
acceptanceCriteria: recordReminder(deps, {customerId}) increments reminderCount by one and returns the new count
It rejects with a typed ReminderAlreadyLoggedToday error when a reminder exists for that customer on the same calendar day, and writes nothing
It rejects when the certificate is not expired
renewCertificate(deps, {customerId, type, validUntil}) records the new certificate and resets reminderCount to 0 in the same transaction
renewCertificate rejects a validUntil in the past with a typed error
Both write audit entries; the reminder entry records the resulting count
Tested against fakes and a fake clock: first reminder, same-day repeat, next-day reminder, reminder on a valid certificate, renewal resetting a count of 3
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ReminderLog schema and appended certificate history
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reminders capped at one per day by the database and certificate renewals preserved as history. Source: tasks/prd-us-06-certificate-reminder.md §US-06.3
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma model ReminderLog: id, customerId (FK to Customer.id), loggedOn day-key, resultingCount
Unique constraint on (customerId, loggedOn)
Certificate rows are appended, never overwritten; the current certificate is the latest by recordedAt
Integration test proves the per-day constraint fires
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Certificate status, reminder action and renewal form
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the counter screen to tell me the certificate is expired and let me log the reminder without leaving the screen. Source: tasks/prd-us-06-certificate-reminder.md §US-06.4
acceptanceCriteria: The counter screen shows the expiry date and, when expired, an unmissable German note that the customer is served this time but must bring a renewal
An 'Erinnerung erfassen' action logs the reminder and shows the new count immediately
After the action, the button is disabled for the rest of the day with an explanatory label
When the threshold is reached with the certificate still expired, an archive prompt appears with an equally prominent option to continue without archiving
A renewed-certificate form captures type and validity end date and, on save, shows the reset count of 0
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: expired certificate through to the third reminder
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec covering the full reminder escalation. Source: tasks/prd-us-06-certificate-reminder.md §US-06.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec with a controllable clock serves a customer with an expired certificate on three consecutive distribution days, logging one reminder each time
Spec asserts a second reminder attempt on the same day is refused
Spec asserts the archive prompt appears after the third reminder and that the customer is not archived until staff confirm
Spec asserts recording a renewed certificate resets the displayed count to 0 and removes the prompt
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
